customer_message: I was charged twice for my May invoice. Can you refund the duplicate?
context: Account: Acme Co, Pro plan. Billing log: two charges of $49 on 2025-05-01 (txn_8841, txn_8842) to card ending 4242. Policy: duplicate charges are refundable within 60 days to the original card; refunds post in 5-10 business days.
reply: Thanks for flagging this, and sorry for the double charge. I can confirm two $49 charges on May 1 (txn_8841 and txn_8842). I've refunded the duplicate (txn_8842) to your card ending 4242 - you'll see it back within 5-10 business days. Your original May payment is unaffected. Anything else I can sort out?

customer_message: Why is my API returning 429 errors all of a sudden?
context: Account: Northwind, Growth plan. Rate limit: 600 requests/min. Last hour usage peaked at 740 req/min on the /v2/transactions endpoint. 429 = rate limit exceeded. Upgrade to Scale raises the limit to 2,000 req/min.
reply: The 429s mean you're hitting your plan's rate limit of 600 requests/min - your /v2/transactions traffic peaked at 740/min in the last hour. Two ways to fix it: add client-side retry with exponential backoff to smooth out bursts, or move to the Scale plan, which raises the limit to 2,000 req/min. Want me to send over the upgrade details, or share our backoff snippet first?

customer_message: I can't log in - it keeps saying my SSO isn't configured.
context: Account: Globex, Enterprise. SSO: SAML connection created but status = pending (IdP metadata not yet uploaded). Until metadata is uploaded, users fall back to password login, which is disabled for this org per admin setting.
reply: I see what's happening - your SAML SSO connection is created but still pending because we haven't received your IdP metadata yet, and password login is turned off for your org. Once your admin uploads the IdP metadata XML in Settings > SSO, login will work right away. If it's easier, send me the metadata file or your IdP's metadata URL and I'll attach it for you.

customer_message: How long does a payout take to reach my bank account?
context: Account: BrightPay merchant, US, standard payout schedule. Standard payouts settle in 2 business days after the daily cutoff (6pm ET). Instant Payout option available for a 1.5% fee, arrives within 30 minutes to eligible debit cards.
reply: On the standard schedule, payouts land in your bank account 2 business days after the daily 6pm ET cutoff. If you need funds sooner, Instant Payout sends to an eligible debit card in about 30 minutes for a 1.5% fee. Let me know if you'd like me to enable Instant Payout on your account.

customer_message: Do you offer a discount for nonprofits?
context: Account: prospect, not yet a customer. Policy: registered 501(c)(3) nonprofits get 30% off any annual plan. Verification required via a valid IRS determination letter or EIN lookup. Discount does not stack with other promotions.
reply: Yes - registered 501(c)(3) nonprofits get 30% off any annual plan. To apply it, just send us your IRS determination letter or your EIN and we'll verify it. One note: the nonprofit discount can't be combined with other promotions. Want me to start the verification so the discount is ready when you sign up?

customer_message: My team hit the seat limit and now nobody new can be added.
context: Account: Initech, Team plan, 10 seats, 10/10 in use. Adding seats is self-serve in Settings > Members for admins; each additional seat is $12/mo, prorated for the current cycle. Requester role = member (not admin).
reply: You're at 10 of 10 seats, which is why new invites are blocked. Adding seats is quick - an admin on your account can do it in Settings > Members, and each extra seat is $12/mo, prorated for the rest of this cycle. Since your role is member, you'll need an admin to add them; if you tell me who your admin is, I can point them to the exact spot.

customer_message: Is my data encrypted? We're in fintech and need to confirm for compliance.
context: Security posture: data encrypted at rest with AES-256 and in transit with TLS 1.2+. SOC 2 Type II report available under NDA. PCI DSS Level 1 for cardholder data. Subprocessor list published at /legal/subprocessors.
reply: Happy to confirm for your compliance review. Your data is encrypted at rest with AES-256 and in transit with TLS 1.2 or higher. We maintain a SOC 2 Type II report (available under NDA) and are PCI DSS Level 1 certified for cardholder data. Our current subprocessor list is published at /legal/subprocessors. If you'd like the SOC 2 report, I can kick off the NDA - just let me know.

customer_message: I exported a report but the CSV is empty.
context: Account: Hooli, Growth plan. Reports export honors the active dashboard filters. Current filter range = 'Yesterday' with a date that has no transactions. Known behavior: an empty result set produces a header-only CSV, not an error.
reply: The export isn't broken - it follows whatever filters are active on your dashboard. Right now the range is set to 'Yesterday,' and there were no transactions that day, so the CSV comes back with just the header row. Switch the date range to a period with activity (say, the last 7 days) and re-export, and you'll get the full data. Want me to confirm which ranges have transactions?

customer_message: Can I get an invoice with my company VAT number on it?
context: Account: Umbrella Ltd, UK, annual plan. Billing settings allow adding a VAT/tax ID and company address, which then appear on all future invoices. Past invoices can be reissued with updated tax details on request.
reply: Absolutely. Add your VAT number and company address under Settings > Billing > Tax details, and they'll appear on every invoice going forward. If you also need your most recent invoices reissued with the VAT number, tell me which ones and I'll regenerate them for you.

customer_message: Someone on my team deleted a project by accident. Can it be recovered?
context: Account: Stark Industries, Scale plan. Deleted projects are soft-deleted and kept for 30 days before permanent removal. Restore is available to admins via Settings > Trash, or support can restore on request with the project ID. Deletion occurred 3 days ago.
reply: Good news - deleted projects are recoverable for 30 days, and yours was deleted 3 days ago, so it's well within the window. An admin can restore it directly under Settings > Trash, or I can do it for you right now if you send me the project ID. Want me to handle the restore?

customer_message: Your webhook stopped sending events to our endpoint last night.
context: Account: Wayne Enterprises, Scale plan. Webhook endpoint returned 5 consecutive 500 responses at 02:14 UTC, triggering auto-disable per delivery policy. Disabled endpoints can be re-enabled in Settings > Webhooks; events from the outage window are retained 72 hours and can be replayed.
reply: Here's what happened: your endpoint returned five 500 errors in a row at 02:14 UTC, so our delivery system auto-disabled it to avoid hammering a failing URL. Once your endpoint is healthy, re-enable it in Settings > Webhooks. The events from the outage are held for 72 hours, so you can replay them and won't lose any data - just trigger a replay after re-enabling. Let me know if you want help confirming the endpoint is back up.

customer_message: I want to cancel my subscription. Will I be charged again?
context: Account: Soylent Corp, monthly plan, renews on the 15th. Cancellation stops future renewals and keeps access until the end of the paid period; no proration or refund for the current month. Self-serve cancel in Settings > Billing.
reply: You can cancel anytime in Settings > Billing, and it'll stop any future charges. You won't be billed again on the 15th, and you'll keep full access until the end of your current paid month - we don't prorate or refund the month already paid. If there's something we could fix that would change your mind, I'm glad to help with that too.
